name: Castillo de Valencia del Ventoso - Sierra de Aracena Natural Park - circular route via Fregenal de la Sierra
name_original: Castillo de Valencia del Ventoso - Parque Natural Sierra de Aracena — circular por Fregenal de la Sierra
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Higuera La Real traverse a landscape characterized by expansive dehesas of holm oaks and cork oaks, which are well-preserved ecosystems. The region features rolling hills and quiet roads, providing varied terrain for cyclists. Its proximity to the Sierra de Aracena and Picos de Aroche Natural Park offers access to diverse Mediterranean forests. This environment provides a consistent and attractive backdrop for road cycling. Best road cycling routes around Higuera La Real The most popular road cycling route is Castillo de Valencia del Ventoso - Sierra de Aracena Natural Park - circular route via Fregenal de la Sierra, a 64.5 miles (103.8 km) difficult trail that takes 5 hours 14 minutes to complete. This route explores the diverse terrain of the Sierra de Aracena Natural Park. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Between borders - the Dehesas de Barrancos — circular from Fregenal de la Sierra, a difficult 86.6 miles (139.4 km) path. This route offers extensive views of the dehesas landscape. Local road cyclists also love the Higuera la Real – Cumbres Mayores Castle loop from Higuera la Real, a 35.1 miles (56.6 km) trail leading through the local countryside and past Cumbres Mayores Castle, often completed in about 2 hours 56 minutes. Road cycling around Higuera La Real is defined by dehesas of holm oaks and cork oaks, rolling hills, and Mediterranean forests. The network offers options for moderate and difficult ability levels. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 190 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Higuera La Real's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Higuera La Real?
A: There are over 25 road cycling routes around Higuera La Real documented on komoot. These routes offer a variety of experiences, with 7 moderate and 19 difficult options to choose from.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when road cycling in Higuera La Real?
A: Road cycling around Higuera La Real primarily features rolling hills and quiet roads that traverse expansive &lt;em&gt;dehesas&lt;/em&gt; of holm oaks and cork oaks. You'll also find diverse Mediterranean forests, especially closer to the Sierra de Aracena and Picos de Aroche Natural Park, providing varied and scenic backdrops.

Q: What is the best time of year to road cycle in Higuera La Real?
A: The region's climate makes spring and autumn ideal for road cycling, offering pleasant temperatures and lush landscapes. Summers can be hot, while winters are generally mild, but it's always best to check local weather forecasts before planning your ride.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes around Higuera La Real?
A: Yes, many routes are designed as loops. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20122197" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Higuera la Real – Cumbres Mayores Castle loop from Higuera la Real&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 35.1-mile (56.6 km) route that takes you through the local countryside and past Cumbres Mayores Castle.

Q: What historical landmarks can I see along the road cycling routes?
A: Several routes pass by significant historical sites. You might encounter the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5145360" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Templar Castle of Fregenal de la Sierra&lt;/a&gt;, a 13th-century fortress with seven towers, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5585855" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cumbres Mayores Castle&lt;/a&gt;. Some routes also go near the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5145368" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza de Toros de Fregenal de la Sierra&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any longer, more challenging routes for experienced road cyclists?
A: Absolutely. For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20122057" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of Saint Michael the Archangel – Jerez de los Caballeros loop from Fregenal de la Sierra&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 83.7-mile (134.8 km) route with significant elevation gain, offering a demanding and rewarding ride.

Q: What natural features will I encounter on the road cycling routes?
A: You'll primarily experience the unique &lt;em&gt;dehesas&lt;/em&gt; landscape, characterized by ancient holm and cork oaks, which are vital ecosystems. The proximity to the Sierra de Aracena and Picos de Aroche Natural Park means you'll also ride through diverse Mediterranean forests, home to rich birdlife including storks and eagles.

Q: Are there any moderate road cycling routes suitable for those looking for a less strenuous ride?
A: Yes, there are several moderate routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19380216" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bridge over the Ardila River loop from Fregenal de la Sierra&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 67.3-mile (108.3 km) route that offers a scenic ride without the extreme demands of the more difficult trails.

Q: Can I find routes that pass through villages or offer places to stop for refreshments?
A: Many routes connect or pass near local towns and villages, providing opportunities to stop for refreshments. The region is known for its local gastronomy, including products from the Iberian pig, so you'll find places to refuel and experience local culture.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about road cycling in Higuera La Real?
A: Road cyclists often praise the region for its quiet roads, the unique beauty of the &lt;em&gt;dehesas&lt;/em&gt; landscape, and the varied terrain that offers both enjoyable stretches and challenging climbs. The well-preserved natural environment and the sense of tranquility are frequently highlighted.

Q: Is parking available for cyclists in Higuera La Real?
A: While specific designated cyclist parking areas are not detailed, Higuera La Real and surrounding towns generally offer public parking options. It's advisable to check local parking regulations upon arrival, especially in smaller village centers.

Q: Are there family-friendly road cycling options in the area?
A: While many routes are classified as moderate to difficult, families with older children or those comfortable with longer distances and some elevation gain can find suitable options. Look for routes with lower elevation profiles and shorter distances for a more relaxed experience.
tips: Bridge that crosses the Río Ardila in what is called the Campo de Zafra, a set of Dehesas spaces close to the important city that has the same name, Zafra.

Valencia del Ventoso is a town located near Zafra on the southern edge of the Zafrense area, already in the foothills of the southern sierras, on land covered with meadows and scrubland, of outstanding natural beauty.

Castle that is at one end of the center of Segura de León and from where you can see beautiful views of the surroundings. The castle can be seen on arrival in the village, on top of a hill and which stands out above the houses of the village.
Beautiful road that descends from the town of Cumbres de Enmedio in the middle of cork oak forests and where it reaches the Centro de Interpretación y Yacimiento Celta de Capote.
